en: Then Moses answered, "But behold, they will not believe me or listen to my voice, for they will say, 'The LORD did not appear to you.'"
zh: 摩西回答：“看哪，他們必不信我，也不聽我的話；因為他們必說：‘耶和華並沒有向你顯現。’”

en: The LORD said to him, "What is that in your hand?" He said, "A staff."
zh: 耶和華問摩西：“那在你手裡的是甚麼？”他說：“是手杖。”

en: And he said, "Throw it on the ground." So he threw it on the ground, and it became a serpent, and Moses ran from it.
zh: 耶和華說：“把它丟在地上。”摩西把它一丟在地上，它就變了蛇；摩西就逃跑，離開了牠。

en: But the LORD said to Moses, "Put out your hand and catch it by the tail"- so he put out his hand and caught it, and it became a staff in his hand-
zh: 耶和華對摩西說：“伸出你的手來，捉住蛇的尾巴。”摩西就伸出手來，緊握著牠，牠在摩西的掌中又變回了手杖。

en: "that they may believe that the LORD, the God of their fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob, has appeared to you."
zh: “這樣就使他們相信耶和華他們祖宗的 神，就是亞伯拉罕的 神、以撒的 神、雅各的 神曾向你顯現了。”

en: Again, the LORD said to him, "Put your hand inside your cloak." And he put his hand inside his cloak, and when he took it out, behold, his hand was leprous like snow.
zh: 耶和華又再對摩西說：“現在把你的手放進懷裡。”摩西就把手放進懷裡；等他把手抽出來的時候，手就長滿了痲風，像雪那樣白。

en: Then God said, "Put your hand back inside your cloak." So he put his hand back inside his cloak, and when he took it out, behold, it was restored like the rest of his flesh.
zh: 耶和華說：“把你的手再放進懷裡。”摩西就把手再放進懷裡；等他把手從懷裡抽出來的時候，手就恢復過來，像身上別處的肉一樣。

en: "If they will not believe you," God said, "or listen to the first sign, they may believe the latter sign.
zh: 耶和華又說：“如果他們不信你，也不聽從頭一個神蹟的話，他們必信隨後來的神蹟。

en: If they will not believe even these two signs or listen to your voice, you shall take some water from the Nile and pour it on the dry ground, and the water that you shall take from the Nile will become blood on the dry ground."
zh: 如果連這兩個神蹟他們也不信，也不聽你的話，你就從河裡取些水，倒在旱地上；你從河裡取來的水就必在旱地上變成血。”

en: But Moses said to the LORD, "Oh, my Lord, I am not eloquent, either in the past or since you have spoken to your servant, but I am slow of speech and of tongue."
zh: 摩西對耶和華說：“主啊，我不是個會說話的人；以前不是，自從你對僕人說話以後也不是；因為我本是拙口笨舌的。”

en: Then the LORD said to him, "Who has made man's mouth? Who makes him mute, or deaf, or seeing, or blind? Is it not I, the LORD?
zh: 耶和華對他說：“誰造人的口呢？誰使人口啞、耳聾、眼明、眼瞎呢？不是我耶和華嗎？

en: Now therefore go, and I will be with your mouth and teach you what you shall speak."
zh: 現在去吧，我必賜你口才，指教你當說的。”

en: But he said, "Oh, my Lord, please send someone else."
zh: 摩西說：“主啊，請你差派你願意差派的人。”

en: Then the anger of the LORD was kindled against Moses and he said, "Is there not Aaron, your brother, the Levite? I know that he can speak well. Behold, he is coming out to meet you, and when he sees you, he will be glad in his heart.
zh: 耶和華向摩西發怒，說：“不是有你的哥哥利未人亞倫嗎？我知道他是有口才的；他現在出來要迎接你。他看見了你，心裡就快樂。

en: You shall speak to him and put the words in his mouth, and I will be with your mouth and with his mouth and will teach you both what to do.
zh: 你要對他說話，把你要說的話放在他的口裡；我必與你的口同在，也與他的口同在；我必指教你們當行的事。

en: He shall speak for you to the people, and he shall be your mouth, and you shall be as God to him.
zh: 他要替你向人民說話，他要作你的口，你要作他的 神。

en: And take in your hand this staff, with which you shall do the signs."
zh: 這手杖你要拿在手裡，用來行神蹟。”

en: Moses went back to Jethro his father-in-law and said to him, "Please let me go back to my brothers in Egypt to see whether they are still alive." And Jethro said to Moses, "Go in peace."
zh: 於是，摩西起程回到他岳父葉忒羅那裡，對他說：“請你容我離開這裡，回到我在埃及的同胞那裡，看看他們是否還在。”葉忒羅對摩西說：“你可以平平安安地去吧。”

en: And the LORD said to Moses in Midian, "Go back to Egypt, for all the men who were seeking your life are dead."
zh: 耶和華在米甸對摩西說：“你要起程，回到埃及去，因為那些尋索你命的人都死了。”

en: So Moses took his wife and his sons and had them ride on a donkey, and went back to the land of Egypt. And Moses took the staff of God in his hand.
zh: 摩西就帶著他的妻子和孩子，讓他們騎在驢上，向著埃及地回去了；摩西手裡拿著 神的杖。

en: And the LORD said to Moses, "When you go back to Egypt, see that you do before Pharaoh all the miracles that I have put in your power. But I will harden his heart, so that he will not let the people go.
zh: 耶和華對摩西說：“你起程回到埃及去的時候，要留意我交在你手裡的一切奇事，把它們行在法老面前；我卻要使他的心剛硬，他就不讓人民離開。

en: Then you shall say to Pharaoh, 'Thus says the LORD, Israel is my firstborn son,
zh: 你要對法老說：‘耶和華這樣說：以色列是我的長子。

en: and I say to you, "Let my son go that he may serve me." If you refuse to let him go, behold, I will kill your firstborn son.'"
zh: 我曾對你說，要讓我的兒子離開這裡，好叫他事奉我。你還是拒絕讓他離開，看哪！我必殺你的長子。’”

en: At a lodging place on the way the LORD met him and sought to put him to death.
zh: 在路上住宿的地方，耶和華遇見了摩西，就想要殺他。

en: Then Zipporah took a flint and cut off her son's foreskin and touched Moses' feet with it and said, "Surely you are a bridegroom of blood to me!"
zh: 西坡拉就拿了一把石刀，把她兒子的包皮割下來，把它丟到摩西的腳上，說：“你真是我的血郎了！”

en: So he let him alone. It was then that she said, "A bridegroom of blood," because of the circumcision.
zh: 這樣，耶和華才放了他。那時西坡拉說：“你是血郎，是因為割禮的緣故。”

en: The LORD said to Aaron, "Go into the wilderness to meet Moses." So he went and met him at the mountain of God and kissed him.
zh: 耶和華對亞倫說：“你到曠野去迎接摩西。”他就去了；他在 神的山遇見了摩西，就和他親吻。

en: And Moses told Aaron all the words of the LORD with which he had sent him to speak, and all the signs that he had commanded him to do.
zh: 摩西把耶和華派他所說的一切話，和吩咐他所行的一切神蹟都告訴了亞倫。

en: Then Moses and Aaron went and gathered together all the elders of the people of Israel.
zh: 摩西和亞倫就去召集以色列人的眾長老。

en: Aaron spoke all the words that the LORD had spoken to Moses and did the signs in the sight of the people.
zh: 亞倫把耶和華對摩西所說的一切話都說了一遍，並且在人民的眼前行了這些神蹟。

en: And the people believed; and when they heard that the LORD had visited the people of Israel and that he had seen their affliction, they bowed their heads and worshiped.
zh: 人民就相信了；他們聽見耶和華眷顧以色列人，也鑒察了他們的痛苦，就俯伏敬拜。
